title: INT.KMP
shortSummary: Field Service Planner — a fleet scheduling engine tracking coffee-machine maintenance cycles.
fullDescription:
INT.KMP is a field-service planning platform I engineered to keep an entire fleet of coffee machines running flawlessly. I built a genuinely platform-independent operator experience in **Flutter**, delivering a single, polished codebase that runs identically on iOS, Android and the web — so technicians in the field and dispatchers at the desk work from the same fluid interface.

Behind that interface sits an isolated, **self-hosted .NET Core** backend that I designed for autonomy and resilience: maintenance cycles, service intervals and machine telemetry are modelled as first-class domain concepts and persisted in **MongoDB**, whose flexible document model elegantly captures the irregular, evolving shape of real-world service records.

My standout engineering effort is a **bespoke authentication and token-management model** purpose-built for field logistics — handling intermittent connectivity, long-lived technician sessions and secure device-bound refresh flows so that a tablet in a basement boiler room stays trustworthy without a constant connection. The result is a robust, offline-tolerant scheduling engine that turns reactive break-fix work into proactive, cycle-driven maintenance.
privacyPolicyMarkdown:
# Privacy Policy — INT.KMP

_Last updated: 2026-05-31_

INT.KMP ("the App") is a field-service planning tool for coffee-machine maintenance. This policy explains what data the App processes and why.

## 1. Data Controller

Salih-Can Özdemir · Düsseldorf, Germany · salih.oez@proton.me

## 2. Data We Process

- **Account & technician identity** — name, email, device identifier, authentication tokens.
- **Service records** — machine IDs, maintenance cycles, scheduled and completed visits, location of service sites.
- **Operational telemetry** — app version, crash diagnostics (anonymised).

## 3. Purpose & Legal Basis

Data is processed solely to schedule, track and document maintenance work (Art. 6 (1)(b) GDPR — performance of a contract) and to keep the platform secure (Art. 6 (1)(f) — legitimate interest).

## 4. Storage & Security

Data is stored on a self-hosted backend in the EU using **MongoDB**. All transport is TLS-encrypted; authentication uses short-lived, device-bound tokens.

## 5. Retention

Service records are retained for the duration of the maintenance contract and deleted within 90 days of contract termination unless legal retention periods apply.

## 6. Your Rights

You may request access, rectification, erasure, restriction and portability of your data at any time — see the GDPR instructions for this project.

## 7. Contact

For any privacy question, write to **salih.oez@proton.me**.
gdprInstructionsMarkdown:
# GDPR Data-Subject Instructions — INT.KMP

Under the **General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)** you hold the following rights regarding the personal data INT.KMP processes about you.

## How to Exercise Your Rights

1. **Right of access (Art. 15)** — request a copy of all data held about you.
2. **Right to rectification (Art. 16)** — correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
3. **Right to erasure (Art. 17)** — request deletion of your account and service history.
4. **Right to restriction (Art. 18)** — pause processing while a dispute is resolved.
5. **Right to data portability (Art. 20)** — receive your data in a machine-readable JSON export.
6. **Right to object (Art. 21)** — object to processing based on legitimate interest.

## Submitting a Request

Send an email to **salih.oez@proton.me** with the subject line `GDPR Request — INT.KMP` and the right(s) you wish to exercise. Requests are answered within **30 days**.

## Right to Lodge a Complaint

You may also complain to your local supervisory authority (in Germany: the State Data Protection Commissioner of North Rhine-Westphalia).

title: INT.Cookling
shortSummary: AI Recipe Harvester — an intelligent parser turning social-media assets into structured cooking instructions.
fullDescription:
INT.Cookling is an AI-powered recipe harvester that rescues great food from the chaos of social media. I built an **AI parsing pipeline** capable of extracting clean, structured cooking instructions from inherently unstructured web data — an Instagram reel, a YouTube cooking clip, a rambling blog post — and distilling it into normalised ingredients, quantities and step-by-step methods.

The app is built for a frictionless multi-device life: **Google Firebase** powers real-time cross-device synchronisation, so a recipe captured on a phone while scrolling appears instantly on a tablet propped up in the kitchen. I orchestrated the ingestion flow so that link resolution, media transcription and language-model structuring run as a resilient, staged pipeline — gracefully degrading when a source is partial and continuously improving the fidelity of the extracted structure.

The outcome is a delightful little knowledge engine: paste a link, and Cookling hands back a cookable, searchable, shareable recipe.
privacyPolicyMarkdown:
# Privacy Policy — INT.Cookling

_Last updated: 2026-05-31_

INT.Cookling ("the App") converts social-media cooking content into structured recipes. This policy explains how your data is handled.

## 1. Data Controller

Salih-Can Özdemir · Düsseldorf, Germany · salih.oez@proton.me

## 2. Data We Process

- **Account data** — email and authentication state managed via Google Firebase Authentication.
- **User content** — the source links you submit and the recipes generated from them.
- **Usage analytics** — anonymised feature usage and crash reports.

## 3. Third-Party Processing

Submitted links may be sent to AI/LLM providers solely to extract recipe structure. No personal identifiers are attached to those requests. Synchronisation and storage are provided by **Google Firebase** (Firestore).

## 4. Legal Basis

Processing is based on contract performance (Art. 6 (1)(b) GDPR) and your consent for optional analytics (Art. 6 (1)(a)).

## 5. Retention & Deletion

Recipes and links are retained until you delete them or close your account. Deleting your account removes your content within 30 days.

## 6. Contact

Privacy enquiries: **salih.oez@proton.me**.

title: INT.DieListe
shortSummary: Real-Time Collaborative Canvas — a multi-tenant list-curation platform built for collaborative planning.
fullDescription:
INT.DieListe is a real-time, multi-tenant canvas for curating lists together — the kind of shared planning surface where every keystroke from every collaborator appears instantly for everyone else. I showcase advanced, **clean Angular state control** here: a single, predictable source of truth feeds the UI, with reactive streams keeping local optimism and server reconciliation perfectly in sync even under concurrent edits.

The technical centrepiece is a **node-based workspace permission hierarchy** that deliberately mirrors the asynchronous sharing model of Google Drive. Permissions cascade down a tree of workspaces, lists and items; access can be granted, inherited, overridden or revoked at any node, and the resolution logic stays correct as collaborators are added and shares propagate asynchronously. I engineered this so that the mental model stays simple for users while the underlying graph remains rigorously consistent — a genuinely hard distributed-permissions problem solved with elegant, maintainable front-end architecture.

title: INT.DriverLog
shortSummary: Travel Expense Audit Companion — a real-time mileage auditor for automated tax & corporate logging.
fullDescription:
INT.DriverLog is a real-time mileage auditor and trip companion that turns the tedium of expense logging into an automatic, audit-ready stream. I crafted clean, ergonomic mobile interface layouts in **Flutter**, designed to be glanced at and trusted while on the move, with one-tap trip capture and clear, compliance-oriented summaries.

Under the hood, **Firebase event streaming** records each trip as a continuous chain of database transaction logs — start, route, stops and end — producing an immutable, time-ordered ledger that stands up to tax and corporate scrutiny. By treating every mileage event as an append-only transaction synced in real time, I ensured that the audit trail is both tamper-evident and instantly available across devices, making month-end reconciliation effortless for drivers and finance teams alike.
privacyPolicyMarkdown:
# Privacy Policy — INT.DriverLog

_Last updated: 2026-05-31_

INT.DriverLog ("the App") records trips and mileage for tax and corporate expense logging. Because it processes **location data**, we treat your privacy with particular care.

## 1. Data Controller

Salih-Can Özdemir · Düsseldorf, Germany · salih.oez@proton.me

## 2. Data We Process

- **Account data** — email, authentication state (Google Firebase).
- **Trip & location data** — start/end points, route, distance, timestamps, trip purpose.
- **Expense metadata** — cost-centre, vehicle, classification (business/private).

## 3. Purpose & Legal Basis

Location and trip data are processed exclusively to generate compliant mileage and expense records (Art. 6 (1)(b) GDPR). Location tracking is only active while you explicitly start a trip.

## 4. Storage

Data is stored in **Google Firebase (Firestore)** with encryption in transit and at rest. Trip logs are append-only to preserve audit integrity.

## 5. Retention

Trip records are retained for the statutory tax-retention period (up to 10 years in Germany) where required, otherwise until you delete them.

## 6. Contact

Privacy enquiries: **salih.oez@proton.me**.
gdprInstructionsMarkdown:
# GDPR Data-Subject Instructions — INT.DriverLog

INT.DriverLog processes **location and trip data**, which is personal data under the GDPR. You have full control over it.

## Your Rights

- **Access (Art. 15)** — export every trip recorded under your account.
- **Rectification (Art. 16)** — correct a mislabelled or inaccurate trip.
- **Erasure (Art. 17)** — delete individual trips or your entire history, subject to statutory tax-retention obligations.
- **Restriction (Art. 18)** — pause processing of disputed records.
- **Portability (Art. 20)** — receive your trip ledger as CSV/JSON.
- **Object (Art. 21)** — disable location features at any time.

## Location Controls

Location capture is opt-in per trip and can be revoked instantly in the device settings. The App never tracks you in the background outside an active trip.

## Submitting a Request

Email **salih.oez@proton.me** with subject `GDPR Request — INT.DriverLog`. We respond within **30 days**.

## Complaints

You may lodge a complaint with the State Data Protection Commissioner of North Rhine-Westphalia.

title: INT.PrintIt
shortSummary: 3D Manufacturing Node Network — a distributed two-way marketplace mapping clients to local additive-manufacturing hubs.
fullDescription:
INT.PrintIt is a distributed, two-way marketplace that connects people who need a 3D print with local desktop additive-manufacturing hubs — a logistics and trust problem that I solved with a rigorous **state-machine architecture** built in **Flutter** and **Firebase**.

Every order is a first-class state machine that advances through an explicitly defined lifecycle:

`created → accepted / rejected → scheduled → printing → print failed (recovery path) → completed → done`

I implemented rigid, validated state transitions so an order can never enter an illegal state — including a dedicated **recovery path** from `print failed` that lets a hub retry or reschedule without corrupting the order's history. On top of this I layered **real-time messaging** between consumer and supplier and **consumer-to-supplier scheduling**, all synchronised live through Firebase so both sides always see the same, authoritative status. The result is a marketplace where the hardest part — coordinating asynchronous, failure-prone physical work between strangers — feels reliable and transparent.
privacyPolicyMarkdown:
# Privacy Policy — INT.PrintIt

_Last updated: 2026-05-31_

INT.PrintIt ("the App") is a marketplace connecting clients with local 3D-printing hubs. As a two-sided platform it processes data for both consumers and suppliers.

## 1. Data Controller

Salih-Can Özdemir · Düsseldorf, Germany · salih.oez@proton.me

## 2. Data We Process

- **Account data** — email, display name, role (consumer/supplier), authentication state.
- **Order data** — uploaded print models/metadata, order lifecycle status, scheduling and messaging history.
- **Supplier data** — hub location, capabilities, availability.

## 3. Purpose & Legal Basis

Data is processed to operate the marketplace, match orders to hubs and enable messaging (Art. 6 (1)(b) GDPR).

## 4. Messaging

Real-time messages between consumers and suppliers are stored to maintain an accurate order history and to resolve disputes.

## 5. Storage & Retention

Data is stored in **Google Firebase (Firestore)**. Completed orders are retained for 24 months for warranty and dispute purposes, then anonymised.

## 6. Contact

Privacy enquiries: **salih.oez@proton.me**.
gdprInstructionsMarkdown:
# GDPR Data-Subject Instructions — INT.PrintIt

Whether you use INT.PrintIt as a **consumer** or a **printing hub (supplier)**, you have the following GDPR rights.

## Your Rights

- **Access (Art. 15)** — obtain all order, message and profile data held about you.
- **Rectification (Art. 16)** — correct profile or hub-capability data.
- **Erasure (Art. 17)** — delete your account; open orders must first be completed or cancelled.
- **Restriction (Art. 18)** — limit processing during a dispute.
- **Portability (Art. 20)** — export your order and message history as JSON.
- **Object (Art. 21)** — object to processing based on legitimate interest.

## Uploaded Models

You retain all rights to models you upload. Suppliers receive only the data necessary to fulfil an accepted order, and model files are removed from a hub's access once an order reaches `done`.

## Submitting a Request

Email **salih.oez@proton.me** with subject `GDPR Request — INT.PrintIt`. Responses within **30 days**.

## Complaints

You may lodge a complaint with the State Data Protection Commissioner of North Rhine-Westphalia.
